rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRACE POLLY VINCENT TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 16, 2006 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Proceedings Of Petitioner'S Petition To Determine Ownership Of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary And Permanent Restraining Order And Attorneys' Fees And Cost
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondent Adrianne Vincent's Motion to Stay Proceedings on Petitioner Grace Marian Plimpton's Petition to Determine Ownership of Property, Damages, Double Damages, Compel an Accounting, Breach of Contract, Declaratory Relief, Preliminary and Permanent Restraining Order, and Attorney's Fees and Costs is GRANTED. No opposition to the motion was filed. On its own motion, the court takes judicial notice of the First Amended Petition for Removal of Custodian, Surcharge, and Breach of Fiduciary Duty filed in In re the Uniform Transfers to Minors Act Account of Grace P. Vincent, Contra Costa Superior Court Case No. MSP19-00926 and the related Register of Actions. (See Declaration of Vincent K. Yu in Support of Motion to Stay Proceedings, filed Oct. 28, 2020, Exs. A & B.) "Where two or more courts possess concurrent subject matter jurisdiction over a cause, the court that first asserts jurisdiction assumes it to the exclusion of all other courts." In essence, the rule renders concurrent jurisdiction exclusive with the first court. (County of Siskiyou v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 83, 89, citing Franklin & Franklin v. 7-Eleven Owners for Fair Franchising (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 1168, 1175.) The two cases need not be identical. "Instead, it is sufficient for the exercise of equitable jurisdiction that the issue in both actions is the same and arises out of the same transaction or events." (Franklin & Franklin, supra, 85 Cal.App.4th at p. 1175.) The rule is designed to avoid "the real possibility of unseemly conflict between courts that might arise if they were free to make contradictory decisions or awards." (Franklin & Franklin, supra, 85 CA4th at 1175.) Here, the petitions in the Contra Costa Superior Court action and this action involve the same issues and arise out of the same transactions or events. The Contra Costa Superior Court first asserted jurisdiction. Thus, assuming that this court could properly exercise jurisdiction over the petition before it, at this time this court must yield to Contra Costa Superior Court's exclusive jurisdiction. This court will hold this petition in abeyance until further order of the court. On its own motion, the court continues the hearing on this petition currently set for January 12, 2021 at 9 a.m. in this department to June 8, 2021 at 9 a.m.in this department and adds to the same calendar a hearing on status of the Contra Costa Superior Court Action (MSP19-00926). The parties are ordered to file-with a courtesy copy timely provided-a joint report on status of the Contra Costa Superior Court Action (MSP19-00926) no later than June 1, 2021. Respondent shall lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, prior to the date of the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 9, 2020. Remote appearance is required, whenever possible, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
